name: Hydrogen
notes: Very small radius (~0.46 Å). Causes severe embrittlement in BCC (especially Fe). Preferentially occupies tetrahedral sites. Critical for hydrogen storage, fragilization in steels and titanium alloys.

name: Carbon
notes: Intermediate radius. Key strengthening agent in steels. Prefers octahedral sites in FCC (e.g., Ni, Pd) and can distort them significantly. Primary mechanism: interstitial solid solution hardening.

name: Nitrogen
notes: Similar to carbon but slightly smaller. Used in nitriding for surface hardening. Strong affinity for Fe-C-Cr systems. Moderate distortion in FCC hosts.

name: Oxygen
notes: Relatively small radius. Highly reactive - typically forms oxides or oxy-interstitials. Can embrittle grain boundaries. Important in welding and surface oxidation.
